summary: Fixes #12486 — Improve UX for 'Copy to' with success dialog

description.text:
<<!-- YOU HAVE TO MODIFY THE TEXT BELOW TO FIT YOUR PR. OTHERWISE, YOUR PR WILL BE CLOSED WITHOUT FURTHER COMMENT. -->

<!-- LINK THE ISSUE WITH THE "Closes" KEYWORD -->
<!-- Example: Closes (link) OR Closes #12345 -->

Closes #12486

This PR improves the user experience of the "Copy to" functionality by displaying a confirmation dialog after entries are copied.

### Describe the changes you have made here: what, where, why, ...
- Modified the `CopyTo` action under `org.jabref.gui.edit` to display a dialog using `DialogService` after successful copying.
- The dialog shows the number of entries copied and the name of the target library.
- This enhances feedback and removes user confusion when clicking the "Copy to" option without seeing confirmation.
- Added corresponding test in `CopyToTest.java` to ensure dialog display works correctly.

---

### Mandatory checks

- [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I license it under the [MIT license](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/LICENSE)

summary: Paste of URL with .pdf into empty entry should get the PDF and import it

description.text:
Filed in issue-melting pot as https://github.com/JabRef/jabref-issue-melting-pot/issues/201

### JabRef version

JabRef 6.0alpha

### Operating system

Windows

### Details on version and operating system

_No response_

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

To reproduce:

1. Copy `https://www.cncf.io/wp-content/uploads/2020/08/OAM-Webinar-V2.pdf` in the clipboard
2. Open JabRef
3. Go to the main table
4. Press <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>V</kbd>

Currently:

![image](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref-issue-melting-pot/assets/1366654/7b3057ef-c68b-4ddd-ab4e-047414833bb1)

Expected:

PDF is downloaded, parsed, etc.

---

Task: Add proper handling.

The code should be related to `org.jabref.gui.LibraryTab#handleNonBibTeXStringData`. Try to debug whats happening when pasting an URL ending with `pdf`. Add handling to that.

Hint: Adapt `org.jabref.gui.externalfiles.ImportHandler#handleStringData`. Check if data is a URL (maybe using org.jabref.gui.fieldeditors.URLUtil#isURL) and ends with `pdf`. If yes, then:

1. Download PDF into the directory `databaseContext.getFirstExistingFileDir(filePreferences)`. (See org.jabref.gui.linkedfile.DownloadLinkedFileAction#execute for other uses).
2. Import the file using `org.jabref.logic.importer.fileformat.PdfMergeMetadataImporter`.

---

Questions:

- Should first a LinkedFile be generated, then download, then parsing, then merge of the two entries? -> Probably not
- It is similar to org.jabref.gui.maintable.MainTable#handleOnDragDroppedTableView. Maybe, we can just call `org.jabref.gui.externalfiles.ImportHandler#importFilesInBackground`. This makes it similar to `org.jabref.gui.externalfiles.UnlinkedFilesDialogViewModel#startImport`.



### Appendix

...
<details>

<summary>Log File</summary>

```
Paste an excerpt of your log file here
```

</details>

summary: Fix PDF URL filename not preserved when attaching to empty entries

description.text: Closes https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/12917 Closes https://github.com/JabRef/jabref-issue-melting-pot/issues/201 Previously, when downloading a PDF from a URL to an empty entry, JabRef would generate invalid filenames like "-.pdf" because the pattern-based naming ([bibtexkey] - [title]) had no values to substitute. This change improves usability by preserving the original filename from the URL when pattern-based naming would produce an empty or invalid result. ### This change: - Modifies LinkedFileHandler#getSuggestedFileName to extract the original filename from URLs when pattern-based naming fails ### Manual Testing I tested this feature manually in the following way: - Created a new empty Article entry - Added file `https://www.cncf.io/wp-content/uploads/2020/08/OAM-Webinar-V2.pdf` using download - Confirmed the file was attached as `OAM-Webinar-V2.pdf` instead of `-.pdf` - Tested with various URL formats to ensure robust filename extraction - Note: I also used other files like `https://www.cs.huji.ac.il/~shais/UnderstandingMachineLearning/understanding-machine-learning-theory-algorithms.pdf` and 'https://www.cs.toronto.edu/~hinton/absps/NatureDeepReview.pdf' ### Screenshots Screenshot 1: This screenshot shows the 2 attached files that have the correct naming convention within the JabRef menu. Here I used the files `https://www.cncf.io/wp-content/uploads/2020/08/OAM-Webinar-V2.pdf` and `https://www.cs.huji.ac.il/~shais/UnderstandingMachineLearning/understanding-machine-learning-theory-algorithms.pdf` Screenshot 2: This screenshot shows that the same 2 files that were added in JabRef, were successfully downloaded in the correct location (location depends on selected user preferences of course) on my local machine. ### Mandatory checks - [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I license it under the [MIT license](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/LICENSE) - [x] Change in `CHANGELOG.md` described in a way that is understandable for the average user (if change is visible to the user) - [x] Tests created for changes (if applicable) - [x] Manually tested changed features in running JabRef (always required) - [x] Screenshots added in PR description (if change is visible to the user) - [x] [Checked developer's documentation](https://devdocs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? If not, I outlined it in this pull request. - [x] [Checked documentation](https://docs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? If not, I created an issue at or, even better, I submitted a pull request to the documentation repository.

summary: Keyword Tooltip/Cleanup for MSC codes added to Keyword Editor

description.text:
Closes https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/12944

Created a JSON file to hold key-value pairs of MSC codes to their respective descriptions/classifications by parsing msc_2020.pdf (which was provided in [link to zbmath](https://zbmath.org/classification/)).

Created MscCodeUtils.java class that implements a method to load a JSON file and converts said file into a Map object.

Created ConvertMSCCodesCleanupTest.java, added to panel, and when user selects preference to convert and saves, the codes are converted to descriptions. Alternatively, when unselected, descriptions are reverted back to code.

In KeywordsEditor.java I added a private variable, mscmap, that calls the above method. Using this map object, I added a condition in the create tag method to check if the label text matches any of the keys. If it does, then we initialize a Tooltip and call install onMouseEnter and uninstall onMouseExited.

## ToolTip

![Screenshot (18)](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c2b8c91e-ea45-4d8a-a28f-1bc21bf6726b)
![Screenshot (19)](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e7fb3384-bad7-40cd-818e-cf7c321f0c3c)

## Cleanup

### Before:

![jabref_cleanup](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a0b8c7e3-230e-4a58-9398-bd1cc864a8c0)

### After:

![jabrefcleanup2](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9c2c1299-16a0-4da2-88d3-2bec4f687373)
